title: Laboratory Associate I
description: PPD is a leading clinical research organization conducting trials across therapeutic areas. You'll perform analytical work supporting pharmaceutical and biotech research.
responsibilities: Perform routine laboratory procedures following Standard Operating Procedures
Prepare and analyze clinical specimens for research studies
Maintain laboratory equipment and ensure quality control
Record data accurately in laboratory information systems
Clean and sterilize equipment following infection control protocols
Support compliance audits and regulatory inspections

requirements.education: High school diploma or equivalent; some college coursework preferred
requirements.experience_years: 0-2 years of laboratory or clinical experience

benefits: Professional development and continuing education support
Employee assistance program (EAP) with mental health services
